title: Postman の 403 エラー：原因と解決策
reasons: The article accurately defines the 403 HTTP status code and correctly differentiates it from 401, providing foundational knowledge.
It identifies common causes for 403 errors in Postman, such as API key scope mismatches, IP restrictions, and insufficient permission levels, which are consistent with general API troubleshooting.
The suggested solutions effectively leverage core Postman features like environment variables and pre-request scripts for managing API keys and validating permissions.
Debugging steps involving the Postman Console are correctly described and align with Postman's official documentation.

Some advice regarding the direct use of external browser developer tools (F12) for Postman debugging could be updated to highlight Postman's more integrated debugging solutions, such as its in-app browser and comprehensive Console, for clarity and precision.

The explanation concerning Postman Cloud's dynamic IP addresses could be more precise by explicitly referring to AWS IP ranges for cloud agents or clarifying Postman's static IP options for monitors, rather than a general 'Postman Cloud official documentation'.

sources.title: 403 Forbidden - HTTP - MDN Web Docs - Mozilla
sources.claim: When a 403 error is returned in Postman, it means there is no access permission to the target API. This error corresponds to HTTP status code 403 Forbidden, indicating that while authentication itself was successful, there is no permission to access a specific resource or the operation being attempted exceeds the authorization level.

sources.title: HTTP 403 - Wikipedia
sources.claim: When a 403 error is returned in Postman, it means there is no access permission to the target API. This error corresponds to HTTP status code 403 Forbidden, indicating that while authentication itself was successful, there is no permission to access a specific resource or the operation being attempted exceeds the authorization level. API keys each have permission levels (admin, user, guest, etc.) set, and certain operations can only be executed with keys of higher permission levels.

sources.title: 403 Forbidden | httpstatus.io
sources.claim: When a 403 error is returned in Postman, it means there is no access permission to the target API. This error corresponds to HTTP status code 403 Forbidden, indicating that while authentication itself was successful, there is no permission to access a specific resource or the operation being attempted exceeds the authorization level. This is a common problem when sending requests in Postman, caused by API key scope, IP restrictions, or a mismatch in permission levels. If the target API has IP whitelisting enabled, a 403 error is returned if requests from Postman Cloud or a local Postman application are sent from an unauthorized IP address.

sources.title: 403 Forbidden Error: What Is It & How To Fix It | Plesk
sources.claim: When a 403 error is returned in Postman, it means there is no access permission to the target API. This is a common problem when sending requests in Postman, caused by API key scope, IP restrictions, or a mismatch in permission levels.

sources.title: How to Fix 403 Forbidden Error in Postman - Apidog
sources.claim: When a 403 error is returned in Postman, it means there is no access permission to the target API. This error corresponds to HTTP status code 403 Forbidden, indicating that while authentication itself was successful, there is no permission to access a specific resource or the operation being attempted exceeds the authorization level. This is a common problem when sending requests in Postman, caused by API key scope, IP restrictions, or a mismatch in permission levels. Occurs when the scope (permission range) set for the API key does not match the required scope of the API endpoint being accessed. Check what scope the API key in use has, and add scopes from the API provider's management screen if necessary. In Postman, check the Authorization header in the 'Headers' tab, replace it with the updated key, and resend the request. API keys each have permission levels (admin, user, guest, etc.) set, and certain operations can only be executed with keys of higher permission levels. Check the permission level of the API key in use on the API provider's management screen. If necessary, generate a new API key with higher permissions and update the key in Postman's 'Environment' or 'Variables' tab before resending the request. Confirm API Key Validity - Check if the API key in use has expired on the API provider's management screen. Confirm key creation date, last used date, and expiration date, and generate a new key if necessary. Open 'Console' at the bottom left of the screen and check the sent request headers and response body. Confirm if the Authorization header is set correctly and if there is any mention of insufficient scope in the response error message.

sources.claim: This is a common problem when sending requests in Postman, caused by API key scope, IP restrictions, or a mismatch in permission levels.

sources.title: Assign role to multiple users in Postman Results on 403 - Auth0 Community
sources.claim: Occurs when the scope (permission range) set for the API key does not match the required scope of the API endpoint being accessed. Check the permission level of the API key in use on the API provider's management screen.

sources.title: Securely using API keys in Postman
sources.claim: In Postman, check the Authorization header in the 'Headers' tab, replace it with the updated key, and resend the request. If necessary, generate a new API key with higher permissions and update the key in Postman's 'Environment' or 'Variables' tab before resending the request. When using multiple API keys, managing each key clearly with Postman's environment variables feature can prevent confusion.

sources.title: HTTP 403 Forbidden Explained: Why It Happens and How to Handle It | by Natarajan C K
sources.claim: If the target API has IP whitelisting enabled, a 403 error is returned if requests from Postman Cloud or a local Postman application are sent from an unauthorized IP address. This is a common problem when sending requests in Postman, caused by API key scope, IP restrictions, or a mismatch in permission levels.

sources.title: What IP ranges does Postman use to send requests?
sources.claim: Especially when using Postman Cloud, it has multiple source IPs rather than a fixed IP address, so it needs to be whitelisted in advance. Notify the API provider of the source IP range listed in Postman Cloud's official documentation and request them to add it to the whitelist. For local environment verification, add your client IP to the whitelist. Send a request from Postman from a different machine or network and check if the same 403 error occurs. If the error only occurs from a specific network, it is highly likely that an IP whitelist is the cause.

sources.title: Is it possible to know the IP address of monitors in Postman? · Issue #9807 - GitHub
sources.claim: Especially when using Postman Cloud, it has multiple source IPs rather than a fixed IP address, so it needs to be whitelisted in advance.

sources.title: Powerful Debugging with the Postman Console
sources.claim: Before clicking the 'Send' button in Postman, check the 'Request Headers' in the Console tab to confirm if the actual source IP is allowed. Open 'Console' at the bottom left of the screen and check the sent request headers and response body. Confirm if the Authorization header is set correctly and if there is any mention of insufficient scope in the response error message.

Confirm if the Authorization header is set correctly and if there is any mention of insufficient scope in the response error message.

sources.title: Error 403 Forbidden when tyring GET method - Help Hub - Postman Community
sources.claim: In the target API's official documentation, check the required scopes per endpoint, IP restriction policies, and permission level requirements. If not listed in the documentation, contact the API provider's support.

unsupported_claims: Postman Interceptor での送信元IP確認 (Checking Source IP with Postman Interceptor): The role of Interceptor for explicitly 'checking the source IP' of the Postman app's own requests is not its primary or most direct modern use; it's more for browser-initiated requests. This section, a heading, implies a functionality that needs more precise context or a better explanation of its relevance to 'source IP.'

2. Network タブでの詳細確認 - ブラウザの開発者ツール（F12）を開き、「Network」タブで送信されたリクエストの詳細を確認します。リクエストヘッダー、ステータスコード、レスポンスボディをそれぞれ確認し、具体的なエラーメッセージを取得してください。 (2. Detailed Check in Network Tab - Open browser developer tools (F12) and check the details of the sent request in the 'Network' tab. Confirm the request headers, status code, and response body respectively to get specific error messages.): While technically possible for older Postman versions or for debugging requests initiated from a browser, the modern Postman desktop/web app has its own Console and an in-app browser with developer tools that are more direct for debugging Postman-initiated requests. Explicitly mentioning 'browser developer tools (F12)' without this distinction could be misleading.
improvement_suggestions: Optional improvement: add official or primary source URLs for unsupported or weakly sourced claims.

Optional improvement: Clarify the section on 'Network タブでの詳細確認' (Detailed Check in Network Tab) to emphasize Postman's internal Console and in-app browser developer tools as the primary methods for debugging Postman-initiated requests, or clearly state when external browser developer tools (F12) are appropriate (e.g., when debugging requests originating from a browser that are then replicated in Postman).

Optional improvement: Refine the explanation of 'Postman Cloudの公式ドキュメントに記載されている送信元IP範囲' to accurately reflect that for non-static cloud agent IPs, users are typically directed to AWS IP ranges, while Postman provides static IPs for monitor users on specific plans.

Optional improvement: Update 'Postman Interceptor での送信元IP確認' to reflect its current primary use cases (capturing browser traffic for cookies/requests) and how it relates to source IP visibility compared to the Postman Console.
Optional improvement: Add official or primary source URLs for unsupported claims.
